title: FHA Loans for First-Time Home Buyers in Florida (2026 Guide)
description: A first-time home buyer's guide to FHA loans in Florida: 3.5% down, 580 credit score, gift funds, down payment assistance, and the step-by-step path to your first home.

Home/FHA First-Time Home Buyer Florida
FHA First-Time Home Buyer Florida

FHA loans for first-time home buyers in Florida

Buying your first home in Florida? FHA loans let you start with just 3.5% down and flexible credit — and they pair with Florida assistance programs to make it even more affordable.

Get My Free Rate QuoteCall __PHONE_DISPLAY__
3.5% down580+ credit scoreGift funds allowedPairs with assistance

Get your free FHA quote

No obligation. See your rate & payment in minutes.

🔒 Your information is secure. By submitting you agree to be contacted about FHA loan options. This is not a commitment to lend.

Thank you!

An FHA mortgage specialist will reach out shortly with your personalized Florida rate quote.

First-Time Buyers

FHA loans for first-time buyers in Florida

For most first-time buyers in Florida, an FHA loan is the most realistic path to a first home. It was built for buyers who have steady income but limited savings or a still-growing credit history — exactly where many first-timers are. You can buy with a low down payment, a modest credit score, and down payment help that, in many cases, covers nearly all of your cash to close.

Here is the honest, no-jargon version of how it works in Florida, what it costs, and how to line up assistance so you are not caught short at the closing table.

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Minimum score580

500–579 with 10% down

Down payment helpUp to $35,000

Florida Hometown Heroes

Gift funds100% allowed

From eligible family

The Basics

What it takes to qualify

Why FHA fits first-time buyers

FHA loans are insured by the Federal Housing Administration, which lets lenders approve buyers who would not fit a conventional loan. The trade-offs that matter most to a first-timer:

  • Just 3.5% down with a credit score of 580 or higher — on a $400,000 home that is $14,000, not $80,000.
  • Flexible credit: scores from 500–579 can still qualify with 10% down, and past bumps don’t automatically disqualify you.
  • Gift funds allowed: your entire down payment and closing costs can come from a family gift.
  • Higher debt-to-income flexibility than most conventional programs, which helps if you have student loans or a car payment.
  • No income limit on FHA itself — assistance programs have limits, but the FHA loan does not.
  • Assumable: a future buyer may be able to take over your low rate, which can help you sell.

What you need to qualify

  • A credit score of 580+ for 3.5% down (or 500–579 with 10% down).
  • Two years of steady, documentable income (W-2 or self-employment).
  • A debt-to-income ratio generally up to ~43–50%, depending on the file.
  • The home must be your primary residence and pass an FHA appraisal.
  • Funds to close — which can be your savings, a gift, or down payment assistance.
The Numbers

What it actually costs

Here is a realistic Florida example for a $400,000 home so you can see where the cash goes:

Home price$400,000

Sample purchase

3.5% down$14,000

Your minimum

Loan amount$392,755

Incl. financed upfront MIP

Est. payment$3,196/mo

P&I + MIP + tax + ins

Illustration only, using a sample rate for demonstration. Rates, taxes, insurance, and mortgage insurance change and vary by loan, county, and borrower — your actual numbers will differ. Not a commitment to lend.

On top of the down payment, expect closing costs of roughly 2–5% of the price (lender, title, and prepaid taxes/insurance). The good news: in Florida, down payment assistance like the Hometown Heroes program can cover most or all of that cash — some buyers close for almost nothing out of pocket.

How It Works

From pre-approval to keys

01

Get pre-approved

Share your income, debts, and goals. We review your scenario and issue a pre-approval letter — often the same day — so you know your budget and FHA loan limit.

02

Shop with confidence

House-hunt knowing your price range and that sellers will take your offer seriously. We help you fold in down payment assistance from the start.

03

Offer & appraisal

Once your offer is accepted, the home gets an FHA appraisal to confirm value and that it meets FHA’s basic safety standards.

04

Close & move in

We finalize underwriting, you sign, and the keys are yours. First-time buyers typically close in about 30–45 days.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

Do I have to be a first-time buyer to get an FHA loan?+
No. FHA loans are open to repeat buyers too. The 'first-time buyer' label mainly matters for certain down payment assistance programs, some of which define a first-time buyer as anyone who hasn’t owned a home in the past three years.
How much do I need for a down payment?+
As little as 3.5% of the purchase price with a credit score of 580 or higher. On a $400,000 Florida home that’s about $14,000 — and that money can come from a gift or a down payment assistance program.
What credit score do I need?+
580 or higher to qualify for the 3.5% down option. Scores between 500 and 579 may still qualify with a 10% down payment, depending on the lender.
Is there an income limit for an FHA loan?+
No. The FHA loan itself has no maximum income. Down payment assistance programs like Florida Hometown Heroes do have county-based income limits, but the underlying FHA mortgage does not.
What is mortgage insurance and do I pay it forever?+
FHA loans include a 1.75% upfront mortgage insurance premium (usually financed into the loan) plus an annual premium of about 0.55% paid monthly. On most loans with the minimum down payment, the annual premium stays for the life of the loan, though you can later refinance to remove it once you have enough equity.
Can my family give me the down payment?+
Yes. FHA allows 100% of your down payment and closing costs to come from a documented gift from an eligible donor such as a family member.
Can I combine an FHA loan with down payment assistance?+
Yes — that’s one of the biggest advantages in Florida. Programs like Hometown Heroes provide up to $35,000 toward your down payment and closing costs and pair directly with an FHA loan.
How long does it take to close?+
Most first-time FHA purchases in Florida close in roughly 30 to 45 days from accepted offer, depending on the appraisal and how quickly documents come together.
